title: Archive Workspaces

posts.author: thebroggi (Random Name)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there

Is there a possibility to archive workspaces?

For many clients we work with this approach: For every projekt our clients start we create a new workspace. Over time there are many workspaces but many of them are obsolete and could be removed from showing up when browsing through all workspaces and only be found when using the search.

This would be a simple but amazing feature.

Best,
Florian

posts.author: Adurm (Adam Cranch)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also in dire need of this feature for similar reasons.

posts.author: tanu (Tanushree Kumar)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be great to archive workspaces that were utilized for completed projects.

posts.author: oliverchem (Oliver)
posts.cookedHtml: adding to this, we create “workspaces” based on each project. clearly we’re not the only ones :). it would be great if there was a way to archive “workspaces” once the project has been completed.

posts.author: Tai_Freitas (Tainá)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey, Monday! Give it a look, it’s so simple! Really upsetting having to archive manually all boards within a workspace.

posts.author: Aedrei (aedrei)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding to the chorus that this would be immensely helpful for agencies / businesses with multiple clients. It’s pretty standard for these types of businesses to assign a workspace to each client, whether its for collaboration or for internal use only.

posts.author: SAnderson (Sally Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also really like to be able to archive obsolete workspaces to keep Monday.com tidy when we no longer need the workspace

posts.author: BenWalker (Ben)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree, We would like to archive at the end of the financial year. Currently we have 24-25 folder sitting there and all the items still appear in searches.

This must be an easy fix?

posts.author: jonmkiwi (Jon Mason)
posts.cookedHtml: Please take notice of this request, we need it
